defs: That which fends off attack or danger; a defense; a protection; a cover; security; shield.
quotes: Let us be backed with God and with the seas, Which he hath given for fence impregnable.
A fence betwixt us and the victor's wrath.

defs: An inclosure about a field or other space, or about any object; especially, an inclosing structure of wood, iron, or other material, intended to prevent intrusion from without or straying from within.
quotes: Leaps o'er the fence with ease into the fold.
notes: ☞ In England a hedge, ditch, or wall, as well as a structure of boards, palings, or rails, is called a fence.

defs: A projection on the bolt, which passes through the tumbler gates in locking and unlocking.
Self-defense by the use of the sword; the art and practice of fencing and sword play; hence, skill in debate and repartee. See Fencing.
quotes: Enjoy your dear wit, and gay rhetoric, That hath so well been taught her dazzing fence.
Of dauntless courage and consummate skill in fence.

defs: A receiver of stolen goods, or a place where they are received.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fenced (fnst); p. pr. & vb. n. Fencing (fn"sng).]

defs: To fend off danger from; to give security to; to protect; to guard.
quotes: To fence my ear against thy sorceries.

defs: To inclose with a fence or other protection; to secure by an inclosure.
quotes: O thou wall! . . . dive in the earth, And fence not Athens.
A sheepcote fenced about with olive trees.

defs: To make a defense; to guard one's self of anything, as against an attack; to give protection or security, as by a fence.
quotes: Vice is the more stubborn as well as the more dangerous evil, and therefore, in the first place, to be fenced against.

defs: To practice the art of attack and defense with the sword or with the foil, esp. with the smallsword, using the point only.
quotes: He will fence with his own shadow.

defs: Hence, to fight or dispute in the manner of fencers, that is, by thrusting, guarding, parrying, etc.
quotes: They fence and push, and, pushing, loudly roar; Their dewlaps and their sides are bathed in gore.
As when a billow, blown against, Falls back, the voice with which I fenced A little ceased, but recommenced.
